descriptionByLocale.de: Mission Sicherstellung der genauen und rechtzeitigen Ausführung von Kunden-Fonds-Aufträgen in den Mutual Funds, Hedge Funds und Private Equity Funds für alle UBP-Niederlassungen, wobei strenge Einhaltung interner Prozesse und regulatorischer Anforderungen. Liefern Sie außergewöhnlichen Service für Kunden, um eine nahtlose End-to-End-Fonds-Bestellung zu gewährleisten. Hauptverantwortlichkeiten Ausführen von Kundenfondsaufträgen über Mutual Funds, Hedge Funds, Private Equity. Gegenseitige Mittel werden in erster Linie über SWIFT über ein globales Verwahrer verarbeitet, während Alternativen (HF/PE) direkt mit Fondsadministratoren platziert werden, einschließlich der Fertigstellung der erforderlichen Abo-/Rededokumentation und KYC/AML. Koordinierte Finanzierung, Abwicklung und Bestätigungen; Verwaltung von Vorfinanzierungen, Kündigungsfristen und bestimmten Bedingungen (Gate/lock-ups). Ihr Profil Ausbildung und technische Fähigkeiten Bachelor-Abschluss in Wirtschaft (oder gleichwertig). Proficient in Microsoft Office-Tools (Excel, Word, Outlook, VBA a plus). Vertrautheit mit Fondsauftrag Workflows und Transfer Agent Prozesse Umgebungen ist ein Asset Personal Skills Think in “Problem–Action–Results” Begriffe; proaktiv und angetrieben. Starke Kundendienstethik; klare und professionelle Kommunikation. Hoch organisiert mit ausgezeichneter Liebe zum Detail; in der Lage, enge Termine zu verwalten und unter Druck in einer hochvolumigen Einstellung arbeiten. Starke analytische Fähigkeiten mit der Fähigkeit, Probleme zu identifizieren, Lösungen vorzuschlagen und bis zum Abschluss zu folgen. Konstruktiv, kollaborativ und engagiert für kontinuierliches Lernen. Adaptierbar, selbstmotiviert und ein Teamplayer. Klangliches Verständnis der operativen Risiken bei der Fondsdurchführung mit disziplinierter Kontrolle und zeitnaher Eskalation. Sprachen Französisch und Englisch: fließend (schriftlich und gesprochen). Sonstige Swiss Residence Kernkompetenzen: Einhaltung der Werte des Unternehmens: Hingabe, Konviktion, Agilität und Verantwortung - Einhaltung von Vorschriften und internen Richtlinien
descriptionByLocale.fr: Mission Assurer l'exécution exacte et en temps opportun des ordres de fonds clients dans les fonds communs de placement, les fonds de couverture et les fonds d'actions privées pour toutes les directions du PPU, tout en respectant strictement les processus internes et les exigences réglementaires. Offrir un service exceptionnel aux clients pour assurer un traitement uniforme des commandes de bout en bout. Principales responsabilités Exécuter les ordres de fonds clients à travers les fonds communs, les fonds de couverture, les fonds privés. Les fonds communs de placement sont traités principalement par SWIFT par l'intermédiaire d'un dépositaire mondial, tandis que les fonds alternatifs (HF/PE) sont placés directement auprès des administrateurs de fonds, y compris l'achèvement des documents de souscription/remboursement requis et KYC/AML. Coordonner le financement, le règlement et les confirmations; gérer les préfinancements, les périodes de préavis et les modalités particulières (portes/verrouillages). Votre profil Éducation et compétences techniques Licence en économie (ou équivalent). Compétence en outils Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook, VBA a plus). Familiarisation avec les flux de travail de commande de fonds et les environnements de processus d'agents de transfert est un atout Compétences personnelles Pensez en termes de problèmes–Action–Résultats; proactif et animé. Éthique solide du service à la clientèle; communicateur clair et professionnel. Très organisé avec une excellente attention aux détails; capable de gérer des délais serrés et de travailler sous pression dans un contexte de volume élevé. De solides compétences analytiques avec la capacité de cerner les problèmes, de proposer des solutions et de poursuivre jusqu'à la fin. Constructive, collaborative et engagée dans l'apprentissage continu. Adaptable, automotivé et joueur d'équipe. Une bonne compréhension des risques opérationnels dans l'exécution des fonds avec un contrôle discipliné et une escalade rapide. Langues Français et anglais : couramment (écrit et parlé). Autres Swiss Residence Compétences fondamentales : Adhérence à l'entreprise Valeurs : Dédicace, Conviction, Agilité et Responsabilité - Respect des règlements et des directives internes

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Investor Protection
jobs.description:
Main Responsibilities 
 The UK Regulatory Compliance Team is primarily responsible for providing timely, risk-based advice, challenge, and enablement to the business on regulatory matters. The Investor Protection Compliance team sits within UK Regulatory Compliance and assists in managing compliance risk across UBP UK, contributing to regulatory change implementation, and helping to maintain effective policies, training, and governance in line with UK requirements and UBP standards relating to investor and client protection. Investor Protection Compliance ensure investors and clients are treated fairly and transparently, topics include but are not limited to; disclosures, suitability & appropriateness, conflicts of interest, complaints handling, marketing and client communications, vulnerable customers, responsible lending, product governance and best execution. 
 
 
• The role holder is responsible for: 
 
• supporting the team in fulfilling the responsibilities set out above. 

• supporting the team and assisting in the management of compliance risk across UBP UK’s business. In providing advisory support, the role holder will liaise with colleagues in the UK Regulatory Compliance team, Financial Crime and overseas offices as appropriate to ensure local regulations and wider department processes are considered. 

• providing advice and enabling support and independent challenge to any areas of the UBP business or any topics as agreed with management. The role holder will also be expected to support business projects and provide cover for other members of the team as required. 

• coordinating team activities, including but not limited to managing the mailbox and query management, new product and service reviews, product and service ongoing reviews and financial promotions. 

• reviewing financial promotions, marketing materials and client facing communications. 

• initial reviews and coordinating the update of relevant policies and processes considering UK regulatory requirements and market and business development 

• assisting with the analysis of regulatory issues arising and implementation of relevant projects and regulatory changes 

• working with and supporting the team, to respond to advisory queries, upgrade and implement policies considering regulatory developments, and contribute to risk assessments, training and internal governance reporting activity as required. 

 
 Role Duties 
 Investor Protection Compliance support and coordination: 
 
• Assist in monitoring external regulatory and business developments and help develop and implement policies and procedures to reflect new requirements 

• Support in analysis of regulatory developments where required for investor protection topics 

• Assist in analysing and preparing for the impact of upcoming regulatory developments 

• Contribute to compliance reporting, dashboards, and management updates, ensuring data is timely, accurate, and consistent. 

• Contribute to improving the process and procedures to meet consumer duty principles and other conduct requirements within the regulatory framework, Treating Customers Fairly and sound risk management 

• Provide stakeholders with sound and timely advice, guidance and support, where appropriate. 

• Assist in the Regular review of Investor Protection related Policies and other written guidance. 

• Assist in evaluating and progressing proposed new products/ services and other business initiatives. 

• Assist in developing and maintaining training, communications, and guidance that translates regulatory expectations into practical steps for the business. 

• Initial review and investigation, where required, of escalations from business assurance relating to; cross border, client-opt ups, suitability and appropriateness. 

• Review of regular reports related to complaints from business assurance 

• Help to identify regulatory compliance risks. 

 Team Management and Collaboration: 
 
• Uphold the UBP values and, by example, support culture of compliance with policies, appropriate risk management and the principle of treating customers fairly. 

• Support management and the team through active participation in regular team and 1-2-1 meetings 

• Partner with business stakeholders to support compliance objectives and business enablement 

• Participate in group projects as appropriate and required 

• Work within the team to help identify areas for improvement and efficiency 

• Support the Compliance team and act as cover for other members where required. 

 Business & Market Knowledge 
 
• Develop and sustain technical knowledge across the products and services of UBP to ensure business is supported. 

• Retain updated personal knowledge of wealth management/private banking market, ensuring that the reputation of UBP is upheld to the highest level possible in terms of compliance and reputation internally and externally and to consider treating customers fairly and good customer outcomes. 

 Internal Control 
 
• Maintain full, accurate files/notes of key meetings and advice using UBP core systems to store. 

• Help log, triage, and track compliance issues, incidents, and inquiries 

• Support root-cause analysis and follow-up actions under guidance from senior team members. 

 The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by management. Specific duties may evolve based on business needs, regulatory changes, and the candidate’s strengths and interests. 
 
 Committee Membership 
 
• None 
 
 Conduct Rules 
 This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are: 
 
 
• To act with integrity; 

• To act with due skill, care and diligence; 

• To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators; 

• To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly; 

• To observe proper standards of market conduct; and 

• To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. 
 
 Person Specification 
 Knowledge, Skills & Experience 
 
 
• Good understanding of UK financial regulations including but not limited to: MiFID II, Consumer Duty, FCA rulebook 

• Compliance qualifications (ICA or CISI) desirable 

• 2 years+ Financial Services experience preferable 

• Wealth management experience is desirable 

• Ability to assess and analyse information 

• Ability to interpret regulation, identify risks and analyse potential control gaps 

• Strong attention to detail and focus 

• Good written & communication skills 

• Numeracy, drafting and record keeping 

• Team Spirit & Collaboration 

• Attention to detail and focus 

• Good project / change management skills

Person Specification Knowledge, Skills & Experience • Good understanding of UK financial regulations including but not limited to: MiFID II, Consumer Duty, FCA rulebook • Compliance qualifications (ICA or CISI) desirable • 2 years+ Financial Services experience preferable • Wealth management experience is desirable • Ability to assess and analyse information • Ability to interpret regulation, identify risks and analyse potential control gaps • Strong attention to detail and focus • Good written & communication skills • Numeracy, drafting and record keeping • Team Spirit & Collaboration • Attention to detail and focus • Good project / change management skills
descriptionByLocale.fr: Main Responsibilities The UK Regulatory Compliance Team is primarily responsible for providing timely, risk-based advice, challenge, and enablement to the business on regulatory matters. The Investor Protection Compliance team sits within UK Regulatory Compliance and assists in managing compliance risk across UBP UK, contributing to regulatory change implementation, and helping to maintain effective policies, training, and governance in line with UK requirements and UBP standards relating to investor and client protection. Investor Protection Compliance ensure investors and clients are treated fairly and transparently, topics include but are not limited to; disclosures, suitability & appropriateness, conflicts of interest, complaints handling, marketing and client communications, vulnerable customers, responsible lending, product governance and best execution. • The role holder is responsible for: • supporting the team in fulfilling the responsibilities set out above. • supporting the team and assisting in the management of compliance risk across UBP UK’s business. In providing advisory support, the role holder will liaise with colleagues in the UK Regulatory Compliance team, Financial Crime and overseas offices as appropriate to ensure local regulations and wider department processes are considered. • providing advice and enabling support and independent challenge to any areas of the UBP business or any topics as agreed with management. The role holder will also be expected to support business projects and provide cover for other members of the team as required. • coordinating team activities, including but not limited to managing the mailbox and query management, new product and service reviews, product and service ongoing reviews and financial promotions. • reviewing financial promotions, marketing materials and client facing communications. • initial reviews and coordinating the update of relevant policies and processes considering UK regulatory requirements and market and business development • assisting with the analysis of regulatory issues arising and implementation of relevant projects and regulatory changes • working with and supporting the team, to respond to advisory queries, upgrade and implement policies considering regulatory developments, and contribute to risk assessments, training and internal governance reporting activity as required. Role Duties Investor Protection Compliance support and coordination: • Assist in monitoring external regulatory and business developments and help develop and implement policies and procedures to reflect new requirements • Support in analysis of regulatory developments where required for investor protection topics • Assist in analysing and preparing for the impact of upcoming regulatory developments • Contribute to compliance reporting, dashboards, and management updates, ensuring data is timely, accurate, and consistent. • Contribute to improving the process and procedures to meet consumer duty principles and other conduct requirements within the regulatory framework, Treating Customers Fairly and sound risk management • Provide stakeholders with sound and timely advice, guidance and support, where appropriate. • Assist in the Regular review of Investor Protection related Policies and other written guidance. • Assist in evaluating and progressing proposed new products/ services and other business initiatives. • Assist in developing and maintaining training, communications, and guidance that translates regulatory expectations into practical steps for the business. • Initial review and investigation, where required, of escalations from business assurance relating to; cross border, client-opt ups, suitability and appropriateness. • Review of regular reports related to complaints from business assurance • Help to identify regulatory compliance risks. Team Management and Collaboration: • Uphold the UBP values and, by example, support culture of compliance with policies, appropriate risk management and the principle of treating customers fairly. • Support management and the team through active participation in regular team and 1-2-1 meetings • Partner with business stakeholders to support compliance objectives and business enablement • Participate in group projects as appropriate and required • Work within the team to help identify areas for improvement and efficiency • Support the Compliance team and act as cover for other members where required. Business & Market Knowledge • Develop and sustain technical knowledge across the products and services of UBP to ensure business is supported. • Retain updated personal knowledge of wealth management/private banking market, ensuring that the reputation of UBP is upheld to the highest level possible in terms of compliance and reputation internally and externally and to consider treating customers fairly and good customer outcomes. Internal Control • Maintain full, accurate files/notes of key meetings and advice using UBP core systems to store. • Help log, triage, and track compliance issues, incidents, and inquiries • Support root-cause analysis and follow-up actions under guidance from senior team members. The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by management. Specific duties may evolve based on business needs, regulatory changes, and the candidate’s strengths and interests. Committee Membership • None Conduct Rules This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are: • To act with integrity; • To act with due skill, care and diligence; • To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators; • To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly; • To observe proper standards of market conduct; and • To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. Person Specification Knowledge, Skills & Experience • Good understanding of UK financial regulations including but not limited to: MiFID II, Consumer Duty, FCA rulebook • Compliance qualifications (ICA or CISI) desirable • 2 years+ Financial Services experience preferable • Wealth management experience is desirable • Ability to assess and analyse information • Ability to interpret regulation, identify risks and analyse potential control gaps • Strong attention to detail and focus • Good written & communication skills • Numeracy, drafting and record keeping • Team Spirit & Collaboration • Attention to detail and focus • Good project / change management skills

jobs.title: Full-Stack Developer - Wealth Management
jobs.description:
• Mission 
 The candidate will be part of the Data Intelligence & Digitalisation team within Wealth Management and will take part in the development of the digital platform for private bankers. The Wealth Management is currently enhancing its digital platform with the objectives to better integrate data and services for bankers and clients. The candidate will have to work in a complex environment involving technical and business challenges with many stakeholders. 
 
• Main responsibilities 
 
 
• Participate in the development effort of the digital platform 

• By integrating existing web services into a new user experience 

• Develop screens and navigation in the intranet Portal 

• Design and implement new features across the existing software stack 

• Define and challenge upcoming features from a technical perspective 

• Navigate through a large set of existing databases and write optimized queries against them 

• Work closely with business and quantitative analysts to ensure that technical solutions are feasible and meet the needs of the business 
 
 
• Your Profile 
 
 
• 3 to 5 years experience

• Excellent computing and analytical skills. In particular, the ability to drill-down in existing code in the absence of any documentation 

• Proactive and user-focused, with a genuine interest in the functional as well as technical aspects of each challenge encountered 

• Full-stack development across .NET 8.0 (C#) and legacy .NET Framework applications, plus JavaScript (Vue.js) 

• Knowledge of web-based application development. Interest in UI/UX. Knowledge of PrimeVue and AG grid will be a plus 

• Broad experience of databases. SQL (Oracle) will be used as well as Entity Framework 

• Knowledge of continuous integration/continuous deployment (ideally with Azure Devops, Ansible, Jenkins and GIT) 

• The ability to adapt to and learn new skills and techniques in a very dynamic and changing environment 
 
 Nice to have 
 
• Worked in an Agile development environment with Jira 

• Knowledge of finance and banking is a plus 
 
 
• Others 
• Swiss Residence 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

descriptionByLocale.it: Missione Il candidato sarà parte del team di Data Intelligence & Digitalisation all'interno di Wealth Management e parteciperà allo sviluppo della piattaforma digitale per i banchieri privati. La Wealth Management sta attualmente migliorando la sua piattaforma digitale con gli obiettivi di integrare meglio i dati e i servizi per banchieri e clienti. Il candidato dovrà lavorare in un ambiente complesso che coinvolge sfide tecniche e aziendali con molti stakeholder. Principali responsabilità Partecipare allo sforzo di sviluppo della piattaforma digitale Integrando i servizi web esistenti in una nuova esperienza utente Sviluppare schermi e la navigazione nell'intranet Portal Design e implementare nuove funzionalità in tutto lo stack software esistente Definire e sfidare le funzionalità in arrivo da una prospettiva tecnica Navigare attraverso un grande insieme di database esistenti e scrivere domande ottimizzate contro di loro Lavorare a stretto contatto con analisti aziendali e quantitativi per garantire che le soluzioni tecniche siano fattibili e soddisfare le esigenze del business Your Profile da 3 a 5 anni esperienza di elaborazione eccellente e competenze analitiche. In particolare, la capacità di perforare il codice esistente in assenza di qualsiasi documentazione Proattiva e focalizzata sull'utente, con un autentico interesse per gli aspetti funzionali e tecnici di ogni sfida ha incontrato lo sviluppo di Full-stack attraverso .NET 8.0 (C#) e le applicazioni legacy .NET Framework, plus JavaScript (Vue.js) Conoscenza dello sviluppo delle applicazioni web-based. Interessi in UI/UX. La conoscenza della rete PrimeVue e AG sarà un'esperienza più vasta di database. SQL (Oracle) sarà utilizzato e Entity Framework Knowledge di integrazione continua/implementazione continua (idealmente con Azure Devops, Ansible, Jenkins e GIT) La capacità di adattarsi e imparare nuove competenze e tecniche in un ambiente molto dinamico e mutevole Nizza di aver lavorato in un ambiente di sviluppo Agile con Jira Conoscenza della finanza e del banking è un plus Others Swiss Residence Core Competencies: Aderenza ai valori dell'azienda: Dedicatione, Conviction, Agility e Responsibility - Competenza con regolamenti e direttive interne

Das Wealth Management verbessert derzeit seine digitale Plattform mit den Zielen, Daten und Dienstleistungen für Banker und Kunden besser zu integrieren. Der Kandidat muss in einem komplexen Umfeld mit technischen und geschäftlichen Herausforderungen mit vielen Stakeholdern arbeiten. Hauptverantwortlichkeiten Erwarten Sie den Entwicklungsaufwand der digitalen Plattform Durch die Integration bestehender Web-Services in ein neues Nutzererlebnis Entwickeln Sie Bildschirme und Navigation im Intranet Portal Design und implementieren Sie neue Funktionen über den vorhandenen Software-Stapel Define und Herausforderung anstehende Features aus einer technischen Perspektive Navigieren Sie durch eine große Menge bestehender Datenbanken und schreiben Sie optimierte Abfragen gegen sie Arbeiten eng mit Geschäft und quantitativen Analysten, um sicherzustellen, dass technische Lösungen machbar sind und den Bedürfnissen des Unternehmens gerecht werden. Insbesondere die Fähigkeit, in bestehendem Code zu bohren, in Abwesenheit einer Dokumentation Proactive und user-fokussiert, mit einem echten Interesse an den funktionellen und technischen Aspekten jeder Herausforderung begegnet Full-Stack-Entwicklung über .NET 8.0 (C#) und Vermächtnis .NET Framework Anwendungen, plus JavaScript (Vue.js) Kenntnisse der webbasierten Anwendungsentwicklung. Interesse an UI/UX. Das Wissen über PrimeVue und AG-Gitter wird ein Plus an breit angelegten Datenbanken sein. SQL (Oracle) wird ebenso verwendet werden wie Entity Framework Kenntnisse der kontinuierlichen Integration / kontinuierliche Bereitstellung (ideal mit Azure Devops, Ansible, Jenkins und GIT) Die Fähigkeit, neue Fähigkeiten und Techniken in einem sehr dynamischen und sich verändernden Umfeld anzupassen und zu erlernen Nice to have Worked in a Agile development Environment with Jira Knowledge of Finance and bank is a plus Others Swiss Residence Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s Values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with Regulations and intern Directives
descriptionByLocale.fr: Mission Le candidat fera partie de l'équipe de Data Intelligence & Digitalisation au sein de Wealth Management et participera au développement de la plateforme numérique pour les banquiers privés. La Gestion de patrimoine améliore actuellement sa plateforme numérique avec pour objectif de mieux intégrer les données et les services pour les banquiers et les clients. Le candidat devra travailler dans un environnement complexe comportant des défis techniques et commerciaux avec de nombreux intervenants. Principales responsabilités Participer aux efforts de développement de la plateforme numérique En intégrant les services web existants dans une nouvelle expérience utilisateur Développez des écrans et de la navigation dans le portail intranet Concevoir et mettre en œuvre de nouvelles fonctionnalités dans la pile de logiciels existante Définir et défier les fonctionnalités à venir d'un point de vue technique Naviguez dans un grand nombre de bases de données existantes et écrivez des requêtes optimisées à leur sujet Travailler en étroite collaboration avec les analystes commerciaux et quantitatifs pour s'assurer que les solutions techniques sont réalisables et répondre aux besoins de l'entreprise Votre profil 3 à 5 ans d'expérience Excellentes compétences en informatique et en analyse. En particulier, la capacité de percer dans le code existant en l'absence de toute documentation Proactive et axée sur l'utilisateur, avec un véritable intérêt pour les aspects fonctionnels et techniques de chaque défi rencontré le développement complet dans .NET 8.0 (C#) et les anciennes applications .NET Framework, plus JavaScript (Vue.js) Connaissance du développement d'applications Web. Intérêts pour l'UI/UX. La connaissance du réseau PrimeVue et AG sera une expérience étendue des bases de données. SQL (Oracle) sera utilisé ainsi que Cadre d'entité Connaissance de l'intégration continue/déploiement continu (idéalement avec Azure Devops, Ansible, Jenkins et GIT) La capacité d'adapter et d'apprendre de nouvelles compétences et techniques dans un environnement très dynamique et changeant Nice d'avoir travaillé dans un environnement de développement agile avec Jira La connaissance de la finance et de la banque est un plus Autres Swiss Residence Compétences de base : Adhérence à l'entreprise Valeurs : Dédicace, Conviction, Agilité et Responsabilité - Conformité aux règlements et directives internes

jobs.title: Business Monitoring Officer

descriptionByLocale.it: Main Responsibilities Business Risk – Assist with implementing the Bank’s Suitability Framework, creating the relevant procedures and guides, providing training on documents and systems relevant to suitability and guiding the Front Office on best practices. Business Monitoring – Assist with implementing procedures and control solutions and monitoring business activities to ensure compliant Front Office & Client Documentation staff and activities, providing assurance on the quality of the controls and tasks performed. Conducting investigations in relation to procedural and control issues and leading associated remediation activity. Role Duties Key Accountabilities General : • Maintain a Group-wide and ‘team’ perspective in project leadership and business support activities, balancing commercial and control requirements whilst recognising location differences where they need to be applied; • Comply with external regulatory and internal policy requirements, including the Bank’s global and regional Directives, Policies, Operating Procedures and controls to meet the Bank’s local regulatory requirements and to protect the business from financial, regulatory and reputational risk; Quality Assurance, Monitoring and Oversight : • Monitor Private Banking and Client Documentation processes and activities, seeking continuous improvement and assuring quality; • Perform assurance testing on key aspects of risk and controls; • Review / Monitor defined activities in conjunction with Risk / Compliance; • Monitor Private Banking and Client Documentation rectification of document deficiencies or completion of remediation project work; • Assist with completing the Team’s Management & Functional Controls; • Review and approve, where appropriate, exceptions to processes and re-tagging of clients; Procedures & Controls Implementation: • Assist with reviewing and agreeing local Directives and Policies; • Review and implement User Guides and Key Operating Procedures for key Front Office and Client Documentation processes; • Assist with implementing and reviewing the Management & Functional Controls (MFCs) for the London Private Banking business; • Agree specifications and provide support for testing and sign off for implementation of PB Portal / other systems enhancements; • Provide input to, or represent, Private Banking in operational / regulatory initiatives or projects; • Collaborate with support functions (e.g. Risk, Operations) on compliance, loss prevention and workplace efficiency; • Find and implement solutions for ad-hoc queries from Private Banking staff. Audits / Investigations and Remediation: • Investigate and report on operational issues / errors and ensure client complaints are escalated; • Provide monthly management information for specified risk items to Risk for presentation at the London Compliance & Risk Committee / Management Committee for management oversight and review with relevant stakeholders; • Assist with the production of client data lists, and analysis in response to audit comments and audit points; • Assist with the remediation of any open audit points to closure; • Follow up on risk issues logged in the risk database owned by Private Banking. Training : • Support the development of training guides on key business processes to assist in improving knowledge and reducing operational errors; • Contribute to / deliver relevant regulatory / internal training to ensure competent and compliant staff and activities; • Deliver relevant induction training for all new staff within Wealth Management. The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by Line Manager and Management. Committee Membership • None Conduct Rules This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are: • To act with integrity; • To act with due skill, care and diligence; • To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators; • To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly; • To observe proper standards of market conduct; and • To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. Person Specification Knowledge, Skills & Experience Qualifications: Completion of UBP Annual Compliance training programme Critical Competencies: skills and abilities required for the role: • Strong understanding of UBP systems and procedures, • Strong understanding of risk management principles and tools; • Strong understanding of local regulatory requirements; • Strong understanding of business processes and associated controls within financial institutions, preferably within Private Banking; • Strong organisational and attention to detail skills, with the ability to handle multiple tasks and meet deadlines; • Strong communication, stakeholder engagement and relationship building skills with key stakeholders; • Strong analytical and problem solving skills; • Strong management and project management / co-ordination skills; • Strong understanding of Microsoft Office Applications, in particular Excel. Competencies and Values Required

Role Duties Key Accountabilities General : • Maintain a Group-wide and ‘team’ perspective in project leadership and business support activities, balancing commercial and control requirements whilst recognising location differences where they need to be applied; • Comply with external regulatory and internal policy requirements, including the Bank’s global and regional Directives, Policies, Operating Procedures and controls to meet the Bank’s local regulatory requirements and to protect the business from financial, regulatory and reputational risk; Quality Assurance, Monitoring and Oversight : • Monitor Private Banking and Client Documentation processes and activities, seeking continuous improvement and assuring quality; • Perform assurance testing on key aspects of risk and controls; • Review / Monitor defined activities in conjunction with Risk / Compliance; • Monitor Private Banking and Client Documentation rectification of document deficiencies or completion of remediation project work; • Assist with completing the Team’s Management & Functional Controls; • Review and approve, where appropriate, exceptions to processes and re-tagging of clients; Procedures & Controls Implementation: • Assist with reviewing and agreeing local Directives and Policies; • Review and implement User Guides and Key Operating Procedures for key Front Office and Client Documentation processes; • Assist with implementing and reviewing the Management & Functional Controls (MFCs) for the London Private Banking business; • Agree specifications and provide support for testing and sign off for implementation of PB Portal / other systems enhancements; • Provide input to, or represent, Private Banking in operational / regulatory initiatives or projects; • Collaborate with support functions (e.g. Risk, Operations) on compliance, loss prevention and workplace efficiency; • Find and implement solutions for ad-hoc queries from Private Banking staff. Audits / Investigations and Remediation: • Investigate and report on operational issues / errors and ensure client complaints are escalated; • Provide monthly management information for specified risk items to Risk for presentation at the London Compliance & Risk Committee / Management Committee for management oversight and review with relevant stakeholders; • Assist with the production of client data lists, and analysis in response to audit comments and audit points; • Assist with the remediation of any open audit points to closure; • Follow up on risk issues logged in the risk database owned by Private Banking. Training : • Support the development of training guides on key business processes to assist in improving knowledge and reducing operational errors; • Contribute to / deliver relevant regulatory / internal training to ensure competent and compliant staff and activities; • Deliver relevant induction training for all new staff within Wealth Management. The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by Line Manager and Management. Committee Membership • None Conduct Rules This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are: • To act with integrity; • To act with due skill, care and diligence; • To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators; • To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly; • To observe proper standards of market conduct; and • To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. Person Specification Knowledge, Skills & Experience Qualifications: Completion of UBP Annual Compliance training programme Critical Competencies: skills and abilities required for the role: • Strong understanding of UBP systems and procedures, • Strong understanding of risk management principles and tools; • Strong understanding of local regulatory requirements; • Strong understanding of business processes and associated controls within financial institutions, preferably within Private Banking; • Strong organisational and attention to detail skills, with the ability to handle multiple tasks and meet deadlines; • Strong communication, stakeholder engagement and relationship building skills with key stakeholders; • Strong analytical and problem solving skills; • Strong management and project management / co-ordination skills; • Strong understanding of Microsoft Office Applications, in particular Excel. Competencies and Values Required

descriptionByLocale.it: Assicurare il rilevamento e il monitoraggio delle transazioni ad alto rischio in una prospettiva AML e in materia di leggi e regolamenti applicabili, nonché di politiche e procedure interne. Eseguire altri controlli di conformità e compiti di monitoraggio. ## Mansioni - Eseguire il secondo livello di controllo sugli avvisi ad alto rischio generati dal sistema di monitoraggio delle transazioni per identificare attività sospette e potenziali rischi per la criminalità finanziaria; - Valutare la completezza e l'accuratezza delle spiegazioni RM sugli avvisi AML e analizzare la plausibilità delle informazioni e della documentazione di supporto sul file; - Eseguire ulteriori chiarimenti e indagini sui pagamenti sospettivi o modelli; ## Requisiti - Laurea in Giurisprudenza, Economia, Finanza, Business Administration o equivalente. - Un’esperienza minima di 2 anni come Compliance, KYC e AML in Private Banking o un ruolo simile; - conoscenza approfondita dei processi bancari, delle operazioni, dei prodotti e dei sistemi, AML e sanzioni; - Capacità di affrontare più questioni contemporaneamente; - Capacità di lavorare in modo indipendente, prendere decisioni e impostare le priorità; - Pragmatic e soluzioni orientate; - Forte capacità analitiche e di comunicazione; - Fluente in francese e inglese; # Cosa# offrono - Swiss Residence: Mandatory ## Core Competencies - Aderenza ai valori dell'azienda: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Rispetto alle normative e alle direttive interne ## Contatto - Nessuna informazione di contatto fornita.
descriptionByLocale.en: Mission Ensure detection and monitoring of high-risk transactions in an AML perspective and in respect of applicable laws and regulations as well as internal policies and procedures. Execute other Compliance controls and monitoring tasks. Main responsibilities • Perform second level of control on high-risk alerts generated by the transaction monitoring system to identify suspicious activities and potential financial crime risks; • Assess completeness and accuracy of the RM explanations on AML alerts and analyze plausibility of information and supportive documentation on file; • Carry out additional clarifications and investigations on suspicious payments or patterns; • Review transaction requests and manage daily operations on blocked accounts; • Periodical review of blockages’ reasons and necessity; • Provide guidance, training, support and relevant information to Relationship Managers on daily basis; • Liaise with relevant stakeholders such as Compliance Market Expert, Business Risk Manager, Legal, Risk, Credit Department, and Operations, to ensure a holistic approach; • Prepare internal memorandums and present the case to the dedicated committee when required; • Provide recommendations for enhancing monitoring processes and assist with Compliance projects and initiatives. Your Profile • Degree in Law, Economics, Finance, Business Administration or equivalent. • A minimum of 2 years’ experience as Compliance, KYC and AML in Private Banking or similar role; • In-depth knowledge of banking processes, operations, products and systems, AML and sanctions; • Capacity to deal with several matters simultaneously; • Ability to work independently, take decisions and set priorities; • Pragmatic and solution oriented; • Strong analytical and communication skills; • Fluent in French and English; additional languages are considered an asset; • Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility. Others Swiss Residence: Mandatory Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

Weitere Compliance-Kontrollen und Überwachungsaufgaben ausführen. descriptionByLocale.fr: Missione Assurer la détection et la surveillance des transactions à risque élevé dans une perspective de LAM et en ce qui concerne les lois et règlements applicables ainsi que les politiques et procédures internes. Exécuter d'autres tâches de contrôle et de surveillance de la conformité. ## Mansioni - Effectuer un deuxième niveau de contrôle sur les alertes à risque élevé générées par le système de surveillance des opérations afin d'identifier les activités suspectes et les risques potentiels de criminalité financière; - Évaluer l'exhaustivité et l'exactitude des explications de la RM sur les alertes à LAM et analyser la plausibilité de l'information et de la documentation de soutien dans les dossiers; - Effectuer des clarifications et des enquêtes supplémentaires sur les paiements ou les modèles suspects; - Examiner les demandes de transaction et gérer les opérations quotidiennes sur les comptes bloqués; - Examen périodique des raisons et de la nécessité des blocages; - Fournir des conseils, de la formation, du soutien et de l'information pertinente aux gestionnaires des relations au quotidien; - Assurer la liaison avec les intervenants pertinents tels que l'expert en conformité du marché, le gestionnaire des risques d'affaires, le service juridique, du risque, du crédit et des opérations, afin d'assurer une approche globale; - Préparer des mémoires internes et présenter le cas au comité spécialisé au besoin; - Fournir des recommandations pour améliorer les processus de surveillance et aider aux projets et initiatives de conformité. ## Requisiti - Diplôme en droit, économie, finances, administration des affaires ou équivalent. - Une expérience d'au moins 2 ans comme conformité, KYC et AML dans le secteur bancaire privé ou un rôle similaire; - une connaissance approfondie des processus, opérations, produits et systèmes bancaires, AML et sanctions; - la capacité de traiter simultanément plusieurs questions; - la capacité de travailler de façon indépendante, de prendre des décisions et de fixer des priorités; - le pragmatisme et la solution; - de solides compétences en analyse et en communication; - la fluidité en français et en anglais; les langues supplémentaires sont considérées comme un atout; - l'adhésion aux valeurs de l'entreprise: la dédicace, la conviction, l'agilité et la responsabilité. ## Cosa offriamo - Résidence suisse : Obligatoire ## Compétences de base - Adhérence aux valeurs de l'entreprise : Dédicace, condamnation, agilité et responsabilité - Conformité aux règlements et directives internes ## Contatto - Aucune information de contact fournie.

jobs.title: Market Specialist Emerging Markets
jobs.description:
• Mission 
 Ensuring that new and/or existing clients to the bank, for the Emerging Markets & Eastern Europe region, are compliant with laws, regulations such as AML, CDB, FATCA, CRS and internal guidelines and standards. To provide market expertise and support to the Compliance WM team, handling the day-to-day spectrum of account openings and mutations for Geneva, ensure that business lines adhere to applicable regulatory requirements (Finma, CDB, etc…).
 
 
• Main responsibilities 
• Analyse all kind of Compliance High Risk and complex cases with the Private Banking activity

• Review account openings and perform checks in databases and searches to identify and mitigate any AML, legal or reputational risk

• Prepare memos and submit cases to relevant Committee/s of the Bank 

• Monitor law and regulatory changes which may lead to change in customer servicing and internal policies, both within the core Market as well as in Switzerland

• Assure that local and Swiss laws, regulations, and internal guidelines are respected in the process of the account opening and modifications

• May be involved in different Compliance Projects

• Act as business partner to other team members and departments of the Bank when required

• Any other task required by Management relating to the Mission of the employee

• Work collaboratively with other Compliance teams within and outside Switzerland

• Provide guidance, Market expertise, support and relevant information to the Front Office
 
 
 
• Your Profile 
• Previous experience of at least 5 years in a similar function with market expertise in the Emerging Markets and Eastern Europe region

• University degree or completed by the Certificate of Compliance Management or equivalent

• Proficiency level of French and English.

• Proficiency level in Russian

• Capacity to manage many tasks at the same time

• Interpersonal skills, very good in negotiation 

• Strong team spirit

• Synthetic and pragmatic, with a good analytical spirit

• Delivery oriented

• Discreet
 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Investments Data Analyst - Temporary

titleByLocale.fr: Analyste des données sur les placements - temporaire

jobs.description:
Investments Data Analyst - Portfolio Guardian 
 12 months temporary mission 
 About COO Investment Management: 
 
• The COO Investment Management department at Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is the operational backbone of the bank’s investment platform. It orchestrates the strategy, governance, processes, data, and technology that enable UBP’s investment teams to deliver high-quality, compliant, and scalable outcomes for clients across discretionary, advisory, and fund management activities.

 
 
• Mission 
 
 You will Work inside the Investments COO Investment Management (COO IM) Office and engage with our Buy-Side stakeholders, Discretionary Portfolio Managers (DPM), Product Specialists and Buy Side Analysts. 
 
 As a Portfolio Guardian, your mission is to safeguard the integrity, consistency, and client alignment of our managed portfolios across their entire lifecycle. You will act as the first line of defense for portfolio quality — ensuring that investment intent is faithfully implemented, risks are understood and controlled, and mandates remain compliant with guidelines and regulations. 
 You will also architect, build, and deploy high-impact software solutions in fast-moving, high-stakes environments inside the Investment Project and Tools team. You operate as a force multiplier across the stack — rapidly assessing the terrain, selecting the right tools, and executing with precision to deliver secure, resilient, and mission-critical capabilities. 
 
• Main responsibilities 
 
 Portfolio Guardian 
 
• Take ownership of risk/breach dashboards and other Discretionary Portfolio Management (DPM) solutions, including end-to-end oversight of underlying data-sourcing processes.

• Create solutions that support Portfolio Managers in managing their discretionary mandates.

• Manage existing discretionary models and datasets, ensuring analytics tools correctly source, reconcile, and integrate data from external providers and cross-functional systems.

• Maintain First Line of Defence (LOD1) risk controls and produce high-quality presentations and reports for DPM leadership and internal teams.

• Administer and maintain databases; collaborate closely with Quant, Performance, and Reporting teams to align models, metrics, and delivery timelines.

• Oversee and improve instrument data quality used in product factsheets (equities and bonds), including validation, enrichment, and issue remediation.
 
 Front Office Tactical Commando 
 
• Develop tactical solutions for the Investment Services floor in commando mode, in alignment with Union Bancaire Privée (UBP)’s technological framework. 

• Optimize processes and tactical tools within Investment Services. 

• Contribute to the analytical and technical analysis of business requirements

• Participate in transversal projects as part of the COO IM team.

• Lead the design and delivery of Proofs of Concept (PoCs) and orchestrate their transition to production, coordinating handover and implementation with UBP IT.
 
 
 
• Your Profile 

• You hold a degree in Economics, Business Administration, Financial Engineering, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or an equivalent quantitative/scientific discipline. 

• You bring substantial experience working with large-scale, distributed datasets, as well as demonstrated expertise in automation and process optimization. 

• You have a strong interest and relevant experience in the financial industry, with a solid understanding of investment instruments and/or applications of Artificial Intelligence within finance. 

• You are proficient in Python development and writing complex SQL queries, with experience using APIs. You have excellent knowledge of the Pandas and NumPy packages and proven experience in project development or automation, data management, and data analysis. 

• You have the confidence and credibility to challenge assumptions, question data integrity, and provide constructive, influential input. 

• You are experienced in modeling and in presenting data with data visualization tools (ideally Power BI). 

• You possess outstanding analytical, data-manipulation, and statistical-modeling skills, bringing strong quantitative rigor. 

• You operate autonomously with a high level of accountability, demonstrating exceptional attention to detail. 

• You are accustomed to managing multiple priorities in a high-paced environment. 

• You are flexible, solutions-oriented, and intellectually curious, with a proven ability to solve complex problems and adapt to evolving priorities. 

• Experience with OMS Aladdin and/or PMS AAA is a plus. 

• French / English proficiency 
 
 
• Others 
• Swiss Residence 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

descriptionByLocale.it: Investimenti Data Analyst - Portfolio Guardian 12 mesi di missione temporanea ## Descrizione La COO Investment Management di Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) è il fulcro della piattaforma di investimento della banca. Gestisce la strategia, la governance, i processi, i dati e la tecnologia che permettono ai team di investimento di UBP di fornire risultati di alta qualità, conformi e scalabili per i clienti in attività di gestione discrezionale, consulenza e gestione di fondi. ## Mansioni ### Portfolio Guardian - Prendere possesso dei dashboard di rischio/breach e di altre soluzioni di gestione discrezionale del portafoglio (DPM), compresa la supervisione end-to-end dei processi di sourcing dei dati. - Creare soluzioni che supportino i gestori di portafoglio nella gestione dei loro mandati discrezionali. - Gestire modelli e dataset discrezionali esistenti, assicurando che gli strumenti di analisi di fonti, riconcilino e integrino i dati provenienti da fornitori esterni e sistemi cross-funzionali. - Mantenere i controlli di rischio di prima linea (LOD1) e produrre presentazioni e rapporti di qualità per la leadership DPM e i team interni. - Amministrare e mantenere database; collaborare a stretto contatto con i team Quant, Performance e Reporting per allineare modelli, metriche e tempistiche di consegna. - Sovrintendere e migliorare la qualità dei dati strumentali utilizzati nei prodotti factsheets (azioni e obbligazioni), compresa la convalida, l'arricchimento e la correzione delle problematiche. ### Front Office Tactical Commando - Sviluppare soluzioni tattiche per il piano di Investment Services in modalità commando, in allineamento con il quadro tecnologico di Union Bancaire Privée (UBP). - Ottimizzare processi e strumenti tattici all'interno di Investment Services. - Contribuire all'analisi tecnica e analitica dei requisiti aziendali - Partecipare a progetti trasversali come parte del team COO IM. - Guidare la progettazione e la consegna di Proofs of Concept (PoC) e orchestrare la transizione loro in produzione, coordinando il trasferimento e l'implementazione con UBP IT. ## Requisiti - Si possiede una laurea in Economia, Amministrazione aziendale, Ingegneria finanziaria, Data Science, Statistica, Matematica, Informatica o una disciplina quantitativa/scientifica equivalente. - Si porta una certa esperienza con dataset distribuzione su scala, nonché una comprovata esperienza nell'automazione e nell'utilizzo dei processi. - Si ha un forte interesse e un'esperienza rilevante nel settore finanziario, con una solida comprensione degli strumenti di investimento e/o delle applicazioni dell'intelligenza artificiale in ambito finanziario. - Si è esperti nello sviluppo Python e nella scrittura di query SQL complessa, con esperienza nell'utilizzo di API. Si ha una conoscenza eccellente dei pacchetti Pandas e NumPy e un'esperienza comprovata nello sviluppo di progetti o nell'automazione, nella gestione dei dati e nell'analisi dei dati. - Si possiede la fiducia e la logica per sfidare le ipotesi, mettere in discussione l'articolo dei dati e fornire contributi costruttivi e influenti. - Si è esperti nella modellazione e nella presentazione dei dati con strumenti di visualizzazione dei dati (idealmente Power BI). - Si trova eccezionali capacità analitiche, di manipolazione dei dati e di modellazione statistica, portando una forte rigore quantitativo. - Si opera in modo autonomo con un alto livello di responsabilità, dimostrando un'eccezionale attenzione al dettaglio. - Si è abituati a gestire priorità multiple in un ambiente ad alto livello ritmo. - Si è flessibili, orientaliti alle soluzioni e intellettualmente curiosi, con una comprovata capacità di risolvere problemi complessi e adattarsi a priorità in evoluzione. - Un'esperienza con OMS Aladdin e/o PMS AAA è un vantaggio. - Francese / Inglese proficiency ## Cosa offriamo - Residenza svizzera ## Core Competencies - Adesione ai valori dell'azienda: Dedizione, Convinzione, Agilità e Responsabilità - Conformità con le normative e le direttive interna ## Contatto - Non specificato
descriptionByLocale.en: Investments Data Analyst - Portfolio Guardian 12 months temporary mission About COO Investment Management: • The COO Investment Management department at Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is the operational backbone of the bank’s investment platform. It orchestrates the strategy, governance, processes, data, and technology that enable UBP’s investment teams to deliver high-quality, compliant, and scalable outcomes for clients across discretionary, advisory, and fund management activities. • Mission You will Work inside the Investments COO Investment Management (COO IM) Office and engage with our Buy-Side stakeholders, Discretionary Portfolio Managers (DPM), Product Specialists and Buy Side Analysts. As a Portfolio Guardian, your mission is to safeguard the integrity, consistency, and client alignment of our managed portfolios across their entire lifecycle. You will act as the first line of defense for portfolio quality — ensuring that investment intent is faithfully implemented, risks are understood and controlled, and mandates remain compliant with guidelines and regulations. You will also architect, build, and deploy high-impact software solutions in fast-moving, high-stakes environments inside the Investment Project and Tools team. You operate as a force multiplier across the stack — rapidly assessing the terrain, selecting the right tools, and executing with precision to deliver secure, resilient, and mission-critical capabilities. • Main responsibilities Portfolio Guardian • Take ownership of risk/breach dashboards and other Discretionary Portfolio Management (DPM) solutions, including end-to-end oversight of underlying data-sourcing processes. • Create solutions that support Portfolio Managers in managing their discretionary mandates. • Manage existing discretionary models and datasets, ensuring analytics tools correctly source, reconcile, and integrate data from external providers and cross-functional systems. • Maintain First Line of Defence (LOD1) risk controls and produce high-quality presentations and reports for DPM leadership and internal teams. • Administer and maintain databases; collaborate closely with Quant, Performance, and Reporting teams to align models, metrics, and delivery timelines. • Oversee and improve instrument data quality used in product factsheets (equities and bonds), including validation, enrichment, and issue remediation. Front Office Tactical Commando • Develop tactical solutions for the Investment Services floor in commando mode, in alignment with Union Bancaire Privée (UBP)’s technological framework. • Optimize processes and tactical tools within Investment Services. • Contribute to the analytical and technical analysis of business requirements • Participate in transversal projects as part of the COO IM team. • Lead the design and delivery of Proofs of Concept (PoCs) and orchestrate their transition to production, coordinating handover and implementation with UBP IT. • Your Profile • You hold a degree in Economics, Business Administration, Financial Engineering, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or an equivalent quantitative/scientific discipline. • You bring substantial experience working with large-scale, distributed datasets, as well as demonstrated expertise in automation and process optimization. • You have a strong interest and relevant experience in the financial industry, with a solid understanding of investment instruments and/or applications of Artificial Intelligence within finance. • You are proficient in Python development and writing complex SQL queries, with experience using APIs. You have excellent knowledge of the Pandas and NumPy packages and proven experience in project development or automation, data management, and data analysis. • You have the confidence and credibility to challenge assumptions, question data integrity, and provide constructive, influential input. • You are experienced in modeling and in presenting data with data visualization tools (ideally Power BI). • You possess outstanding analytical, data-manipulation, and statistical-modeling skills, bringing strong quantitative rigor. • You operate autonomously with a high level of accountability, demonstrating exceptional attention to detail. • You are accustomed to managing multiple priorities in a high-paced environment. • You are flexible, solutions-oriented, and intellectually curious, with a proven ability to solve complex problems and adapt to evolving priorities. • Experience with OMS Aladdin and/or PMS AAA is a plus. • French / English proficiency • Others • Swiss Residence Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives
descriptionByLocale.de: Investimenti Data Analyst - Portfolio Guardian 12 mesi di missione temporanea Â Beschreibung La COO Investment Management di Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) è il fulcro operativo della piattaforma di investimento della banca. Gestisce la strategia, la Governance, i processi, i dati e la tecnologia che permitono ai team di investimento di UBP di fornire risultati di alta qualità, conformi e scalabili per i clienti in attività di gestione discrezionale, consulenza e gestione di fondi. Mansioni ### Portfolio Guardian - Prendere besitzeno dei dashboard di rischio/breach e di altre soluzioni di gestione discrezionale del portafoglio (DPM), compresa la Aufsichte end-to-end dei processi di sourcing dei dati sottostanti. - Creare soluzioni che supportino i gestori di portafoglio nella gestione dei loro mandati discrezionali. - Gestire modelli e dataset discrezionali esistenti, assicurando che gli strumenti di analisi correttamente fonti, riconcilino e integrino i dati provenienti da fornitori Esterni e sistemi cross-funzionali. - Mantenere i controlli di rischio di prima linea (LOD1) e produrre presentazioni e rapporti di alta qualità per la guide DPM e i team interni. - Amministrare e mantenere Datenbank; Zusammenarbeit ein Stretto contatto con i team Quant, Performance e Reporting per allineare modelli, metriche e tempistiche di consegna. - Sovrintendere e migliorare la qualità dei dati strumentali utilizzati nei product factsheets (azioni e obbligazioni), compresa la convalida, l'arricchimento e la correzione delle problematiche. ### Front Office Tactical Commando - Sviluppare soluzioni tattiche per il piano di Investment Services in modalità commando, in allineamento con il quadro tecnologico di Union Bancaire Privée (UBP). - Ottimizzare processi e strumenti tattici all'interno di Investment Services. - Alle'analisi tecnica e analitica dei requisiti aziendali - Partecipare a progetti trasversali come parte del team COO IM. - Guidare la progettazione e la consegna di Proofs of Concept (PoC) e orchestrare la loro transizione in produzione, coordinando il trasferimento e l'implementazione con UBP IT. Requisiti - Si possiede una laurea in Economia, Amministrazione aziendale, Ingegneria finanziaria, Data Science, Statistica, Matematica, Informatica o una disciplina quantitativa/scientifica gleichwertige. - Si porta una sostanziale esperienza lavorativa con dataset distribuiti su larga scala, nonché una comprovata esperienza nell'automazione e nell'ottimizzazione dei processi. - Si ha un forte interesse e un'esperienza rilevante nel settore finanziario, con una solida comprensione degli strumenti di investimento e/o delle applicazioni dell'intelligenza künstliche in ambito finanziario. - Si è esperti nello sviluppo Python e nella scrittura di query SQL complesse, con esperienza nell'utilizzo di API. Si ha una conoscenza eccellente dei pacchetti Pandas e NumPy e un'esperienza comprovata nello sviluppo di progetti o nell'automazione, nella gestione dei dati e nell'analisi dati. - Si possiede la fiducia e la credibilità per sfidare le ipotesi, mettere in Discussione l'integrità dei dati e fornire trägt costruttivi e influenti. - Si è esperti nella modellazione e nella presentazione dei dati con strumenti di visualizzazione dei dati (idealmente Power BI). - Si possiedono eccezionali capacità analitiche, di manipolazione dei dati e di modellazione statistica, portando una forte rigore quantitativo. - Si Oper in modo autonomo con un alto livello di responsabilità, dimostrando un'eccezionale attenzione al dettaglio. - Si è abituati a gestire priorità multiple in un ambiente ad alto ritmo. - Si è flessibili, orientati alle soluzioni e intellettualmente virtuali, mit una comprovata capacità di risolvere problemi complessi e adattarsi a priorità in evoluzione. - Un'esperienza con OMS Aladdin e/o PMS AAA è un vantaggio. - Francese / Inglese proficiency Cosa offriamo - Residenza svizzera / Core Competencies - Adesione ai valori dell'azienda: Dedizione, Convinzione, Agilità e Responsabilità - Conformità con le normative e le direttive internation Contatto - Non specificato

jobs.title: Central Vault Operations Manager

titleByLocale.it: Responsabile delle operazioni del vault centrale
titleByLocale.fr: Directeur des opérations de la Vault centrale

jobs.description:
Mission 
The Central Vault officer is responsible for the management, security and operations of the precious metals vault and physical certificates including security and traceability of all assets entering and leaving the vault. He is working within our institution procedural guidelines to ensure a high quality of service is consistently maintained, with optimum efficiency and all standard operating procedures and Key Performance Indicators being adhered to. He will be working closely with internal management on the strategic global growth of our projects; offering products and services combined into innovative offerings and operational knowledge.
 Essential Duties 
 Vault main tasks 
- Places, and fulfills orders from the Private Banking / Clients to maintain required levels of precious metal available in the vault
- Ensure physical delivery to Relationship manager and Clients
- Handling receipts and deliveries of physical securities and metals
- Maintains internal procedures up to date in regards with the security and regulations of the Bank’s vault. 
- Performs inventory maintenance, verification and accounting entries with reconciliation department
- Performs, confirms and maintains banking insurances according to the vault valuation.
 Trading tasks 
- Processes, monitors and validates precious metals and private placement 
- Ensure good receipt of Private Equity subscription and redemption proceeds with external funds managers
- Reconciliation and monitoring of holdings, entitlements determination, pending positions 
- Ensure daily proper record-keeping and development of new activities/business with external providers in regards with vault activities.
 
 Supervisory Responsibility 
 - Coordinates and manages Vault activities at all bank branches
- Ensure allocation to client accounts and physical securities settlement 
- Precious metals receiving - Receives and verifies vault deposits, inspects the integrity of bags and seals and notifies management of exceptions.
- Ensure follow-up with private company to maintain self-evaluation of private equity
- Perform procedures for opening and closing of operations, including vault, alarm and door duties
- Ensure that security/control procedures are correctly performed in accordance with established policies
 Specific competencies 
- Strong knowledge on financial instruments (equities, bonds, warrants, private placements, restricted securities, funds and other), and their operating flows 
- Sense of risk associated with the delivery and receipt of securities
- Flexibility, adaptation to change
- Strong analytical skills with an ability to identify problems and propose solutions.
- Sense of priorities and capacity to take decisions 
- Consistently comply with internal & external regulation
 Skills and Abilities 
- Technical knowledge of detailed domain (Compliance and legal competencies appreciated)
- CFC bank or equivalent
- Fluency in English and French
- Experience of at least 5 years in a similar position
- Discretion and excellent presentation
- MS Office / Banking software / Swift
 Business daily contacts 
- Clients
- Private Banking
- Operations department / Treasury & Trading
- Compliance department / Legal department
- IT
- Banks / Transfer Agents/ Brokers / Custodians / Funds managers
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

descriptionByLocale.it: Missione L'ufficiale Central Vault è responsabile per la gestione, la sicurezza e le operazioni dei preziosi metalli volte e certificati fisici, tra cui la sicurezza e la tracciabilità di tutti i beni che entrano e lasciano la volta. Sta lavorando all'interno delle nostre linee guida procedurali di istituzione per garantire un'alta qualità del servizio è costantemente mantenuto, con l'efficienza ottimale e tutte le procedure operative standard e Key Performance Indicators essere rispettato. Sta lavorando a stretto contatto con la gestione interna sulla crescita strategica globale dei nostri progetti; offrendo prodotti e servizi combinati in offerte innovative e conoscenze operative. Obbligazioni essenziali Vault compiti principali - Luoghi, e adempie ordini dal Private Banking / Clienti per mantenere i livelli richiesti di metallo prezioso disponibili nella volta - Assicurare la consegna fisica a Relationship manager e clienti - Mantenere ricevute e consegne di titoli fisici e metalli - Mantiene procedure interne fino ad oggi in relazione alla sicurezza e ai regolamenti della volta della Banca. - Esegue la manutenzione dell'inventario, la verifica e le voci contabili con il reparto di riconciliazione - Esecuzioni, conferma e mantiene le assicurazioni bancarie secondo la valutazione della volta. Attività di trading - Processi, monitori e convalida metalli preziosi e collocamento privato - Assicurare una buona ricezione dell'abbonamento Private Equity e dei proventi di redenzione con i gestori di fondi esterni - Riconciliazione e monitoraggio delle partecipazioni, determinazione dei diritti, posizioni in sospeso - Assicurare la corretta conservazione quotidiana e lo sviluppo di nuove attività/business con fornitori esterni in materia di attività a volta. Responsabilità di vigilanza - Coordina e gestisce le attività di Vault in tutti i rami bancari - Assicurare l'allocazione ai conti del cliente e la liquidazione dei titoli fisici - Preziosi metalli riceventi - Riceve e verifica i depositi a volta, ispeziona l'integrità di borse e sigilli e notifica la gestione delle eccezioni. - Assicurare il follow-up con l'azienda privata per mantenere l'autovalutazione dell'equità privata - Esegui procedure per l'apertura e la chiusura delle operazioni, compresi i dazi a volta, allarme e porta - Assicurarsi che le procedure di sicurezza/controllo siano eseguite correttamente in conformità con le politiche stabilite Competenze specifiche - Forte conoscenza degli strumenti finanziari (equità, obbligazioni, garanzie, collocamenti privati, titoli ristretti, fondi e altro), e i flussi operativi) e i loro flussi operativi - Senso di rischio associato all'adattamento a soluzioni analitiche - Sense delle priorità e della capacità di prendere decisioni - Coerentemente rispettare la regolamentazione interna ed esterna Competenze e competenze - Conoscenza tecnica del dominio dettagliato (Compliance e competenze legali apprezzate) - CFC banca o equivalente - Fluency in inglese e francese - Esperienza di almeno 5 anni in una posizione simile - Discrezione e presentazione eccellente - MS Office / Software bancario / Swift Business contatti giornalieri - Clienti - Private Banking - Dipartimento operazioni
